steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Teaching people to speak dishonestly is the claim’s core clue; the quotation must show sophists instructing or praising dishonest speech.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C quotes Strepsiades wanting to become “a fabricator of falsehoods, inventive of words … a dissembler,” which directly shows sophists teaching dishonest speech.
The other choices either encourage studying sophistry, celebrate cleverness, or ask about memory, none of which illustrate the dishonesty claim.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
